text: Nine months after the deadly and destructive Palisades fire, an arson suspect is in custody tonight. A 29-year-old man accused of intentionally starting the fire that would kill 12 people and destroy thousands of homes. Investigators say the suspect set the fire on New Year's Day, that would later reignite and become the Palisades fire, one of the most destructive fires in California history. Rick Chambers is live in the Palisades with the latest on the investigation and the arrest. Rick? Yeah. Sure. The suspect was arrested yesterday in Florida, appeared briefly in an Orlando courtroom this morning. We expect to see him again in court sometime tomorrow. He lived here locally, though, when the fire broke out, and he has been a person of interest pretty much ever since. He is charged with starting the Palisades fire. 29-year-old Jonathan Rinderneck is in custody tonight, accused of sparking the flames that charred the Palisades. We will prove beyond a reasonable doubt that he intentionally started this fire. The feds maintain Rinderneck was working as an Uber driver 10 months ago and dropped off riders close to where the fire originated. He also lived nearby at the time. This is New Year's Eve. So it was around midnight, and he went up to the hilltop. And at some point up there, around 12:12 a.m., he ignited a fire. It was the Lockman Fire, a small one up at the Temescal Trailhead. A federal complaint claims that Rinderneck's phone was traced to that area that night, just seconds, in fact, after the flames erupted, and his car was seen parked there. Rinderneck also repeatedly called 911 from the hilltop, allegedly to set up an alibi. And when firefighters arrived, Rinderneck allegedly followed them, videotaped them, and even asked if he could help them. A brush fire was quickly extinguished on the surface at least, but then simmered underground for six days. The fire was a holdover fire.
